question: Is the text "ANCIENT ATLANTIS CIVILIZATION RUINS ENTRANCE — UNAUTHORIZED PERSONNEL STRICTLY PROHIBITED" spelled correctly (including capitalization and the em dash)?
Is the "ANCIENT ATLANTIS..." text formed by naturally curving coral branches as a ruin-like inscription?
Is the text "CURRENT DIVE DEPTH: 11,034 METERS — EXTERNAL WATER PRESSURE APPROACHING HULL STRUCTURAL LIMIT" spelled correctly (including numbers, punctuation, and the em dash)?
Is the "CURRENT DIVE DEPTH..." text formed by a string of rising bubbles?
Is the text "SPECIMEN A-78 ANALYSIS REPORT: ORGANISM EXHIBITS HIGH-INTENSITY BLUE BIOLUMINESCENT RESPONSE" spelled correctly (including hyphens and colon)?
Is the "SPECIMEN A-78..." text formed by gathering fluorescent plankton?
Is the text "DEEP SEA EXPEDITION WARNING: COLOSSAL SQUID ACTIVITY DETECTED AHEAD — MAINTAIN ABSOLUTE SILENCE" spelled correctly?
Is the "DEEP SEA EXPEDITION WARNING..." text rendered with seaweed woven like a calligraphic font?
Is the text "CURRENT POSITION: LATITUDE 32°15'N, LONGITUDE 64°42'W — SIGNAL STABLE" spelled correctly (including degree symbols and coordinates)?
Is the "CURRENT POSITION..." text constructed from shell fragments pieced together?
Is the text "ECOLOGICAL OBSERVATION LOG: FISH SCHOOL EVADING PREDATOR — DEFENSIVE BAIT BALL FORMATION DETECTED" spelled correctly?
Is the "ECOLOGICAL OBSERVATION LOG..." text arranged by a school of small fish?
Is the text "EMERGENCY DISTRESS SIGNAL: ESCAPE POD OXYGEN RESERVES AT 15% — REQUESTING IMMEDIATE RESCUE" spelled correctly (including percentage sign)?
Is the "EMERGENCY DISTRESS SIGNAL..." text carved into fine seabed sand by currents?
Is the text "ACTIVE SONAR SYSTEM ENGAGED — INITIATING COMPREHENSIVE SEABED TERRAIN SCANNING PROTOCOL" spelled correctly?
Is the "ACTIVE SONAR SYSTEM..." text formed by light spots refracted from the surface onto a rock wall?
Are all eight required text instances present (coral inscription, bubbles parameters, plankton analysis, seaweed warning, shell coordinates, fish log, sand distress signal, rock-wall startup text)?
Are all text glyphs and characters intact, legible, and free from garbling or missing strokes?
Do the text elements blend naturally with the underwater environment (perspective, lighting, and occlusion) rather than looking like flat overlays?
Is the image rendered in a deep sea fantasy illustration style (dreamy, recognizable, not a photograph)?
Does the scene correctly depict an underwater coral city with soft water ripples, floating bubbles, and bioluminescent illumination?
Are there any unnatural visual artifacts (e.g., ghosting, smudging, or random noise) surrounding the text areas?
